text.title: Rails ActiveRecord Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the ActiveRecord methodology end-to-end.

step.name: identify-hot-path
step.action: Pick endpoints / jobs where AR > 30 % of request time, or where N+1 is detected. Target those first.

step.name: extract-query-object
step.action: Create `app/queries/<Domain>::<Name>Query`. Accept relation in `initialize(relation = Model.all)`. Add chainable methods returning `self`. Expose terminal `results` returning the relation.
step.decision-gate: If Query Object exceeds 10 chainable methods, split per use case per `query-object-god-class` antipattern.

step.name: eager-load-at-boundary
step.action: Controllers call `Query.new.scope_a.scope_b.results.includes(:organization, posts: :tags)`. Views consume already-loaded associations.
step.decision-gate: If a partial triggers a query, BLOCK per `eager-load-at-boundary` rule.

step.name: bulk-with-find-each
step.action: Replace `Model.all.each` with `Model.find_each` (batch 1000) or `in_batches(of: N)`. Project narrow columns when exporting.

step.name: wire-n-plus-one-gate
step.action: Install Prosopite (or Bullet in CI mode). Configure RSpec / Minitest to raise on N+1. Add the gate to required CI checks.
step.decision-gate: If specs reintroduce N+1, BLOCK per `n-plus-one-gate-in-ci` rule.
